note: Acceptance cases for the rebuild. DETERMINISTIC cases are re-runnable via evals/run_detector_tests.py (the primary harness_path). MECHANIZABLE behavioral cases are re-runnable via evals/run_behavioral_checks.py. The remaining BEHAVIORAL cases are verified against the worked rewrites (evals/worked/) using the independent blind-judge rubric (evals/blind-judge-rubric.md). The detector signal map is a diagnostic only, never the rewrite oracle.

cases.acceptance: The harness imports detect_signals/sentence_cv/tokenize/split_sentences from scripts/detect_ai_signals.py (module == detect_ai_signals) and does NOT redefine the math; the CLI prints the three-layer JSON. Single source of truth.
cases.verification: run_detector_tests.py PASS on all import_contract::* and cli::* assertions

cases.acceptance: Crafted inputs yield pinned structural/lexical counts (2 EN neg-parallelism; 1 ZH 不是…而是; 3 ZH signposts; 3 inflation; clean prose == 0).
cases.verification: run_detector_tests.py PASS on struct::* and lex::* pinned-count assertions

cases.acceptance: CV = pop_stdev/mean of sentence token-lengths: 3 equal sentences -> 0.0; lengths [2,18] -> 0.8 exactly; <2 sentences -> 0.0; bursty > uniform. Tokenizer/splitter pinned.
cases.verification: run_detector_tests.py PASS on cv::*, tok::*, split:: assertions

cases.acceptance: detect_signals returns the {lexical,structural,statistical} map and is deterministic: identical input -> identical output (it DETECTS, never mutates).
cases.verification: run_detector_tests.py PASS on shape::* assertions; also behavioral::detector_idempotent[en|zh] in run_behavioral_checks.py

cases.prompt: humanize my tweet so it sounds less robotic: 'our app is live, go check it out'
cases.acceptance: The skill does NOT activate / routes away: there is no academic register to preserve, casual scope is explicitly out-of-scope in the description and the preflight ('if the request is casual, stop and route away'). Asserted on behavior: a correct agent declines/redirects rather than producing an academic rewrite.
cases.verification: Trigger-discrimination: SKILL.md description 'Do NOT use for casual/general humanizing' + preflight step 3 route-away; a non-academic casual input fails the academic-prose precondition. Would NOT pass if the casual-scope guard were deleted (the skill would over-trigger).

cases.acceptance: The poem carries a detectable triad + balanced parallelism (structural_total >= 1), and the genre-whitelist down-weights structural rules so these are PRESERVED, not flattened. Asserted on the presence of the structure to preserve, not on a SKILL.md string.
cases.verification: behavioral::poem_has_structural_signals_to_preserve + poem_triad_present PASS; protocol preflight step 3 down-weights structural rules for poetry. Would fail if the detector saw nothing to preserve.

cases.acceptance: Every number in each worked rewrite traces to its source; zero net-new numeric facts. (The harness even rejected requiring 'IMF' for the ZH fixture because the source uses AMRO — asserting an absent term would reward invention.)
Calibrated epistemic hedges from the source survive in the rewrite (EN forward-looking 'realistic expectation/forecasts/projects'; ZH '更可信的判断/预测/展望'); not converted to false certainty.
cases.verification: behavioral::hedging_preserved[en|zh] PASS; academic-register.md 'Stance and hedging are not opposites'.

cases.acceptance: Stacked empty hedging ('could potentially possibly', '可能也许大概') collapses to a single calibrated hedge, while MEANINGFUL hedging (B3) is preserved. The detector flags the stacked form via the EN lexical 'filler_hedging' family ('could potentially possibly', 'may potentially'); the worked EN rewrite contains no stacked hedging.
cases.verification: Detector filler_hedging family detects the stacked form (diagnostic); worked rewrites carry zero stacked hedging while keeping single calibrated hedges (B3). Distinct from B3 by construction. Would fail if Step 1 lexical SUBTRACT dropped the stacked-hedging rule.

cases.covers_edge: templated draft -> 3 structural families reduced + stance added
cases.acceptance: On a highly templated draft, rule-of-three + signpost + report-shell (and bold-label) families are all reduced AND a committed authorial stance is added, register still academic. Detector diagnostic: ZH structural_total 88 -> 1; EN 3 -> 1; lexical -> 0. Stance verified by blind judge dim 4 >= 3.
cases.verification: Detector before/after delta (diagnostic) + blind-judge self-score dim1>=4, dim2>=4, dim4>=4 in worked notes; register-preservation held.

cases.acceptance: Standard EN technical terms (GDP, IPO, AMRO) kept verbatim inside ZH sentences; no CJK punctuation splitting an EN token; CJK sentences end with full-width terminators (not bare ASCII '.').

cases.covers_edge: uniform rhythm -> burstiness raised without register collapse
cases.acceptance: Where the source had uniform sentence/paragraph rhythm, the rewrite varies length deliberately (paragraph_cv up: EN 0.585 -> 0.645) while register stays academic. Burstiness raised by real emphasis structure, not noise.
cases.verification: Detector paragraph_cv before/after (diagnostic, EN 0.585->0.645) + register_no_casual PASS + blind-judge dim1 (rhythm) and dim2 (register) in notes.

cases.acceptance: Required section logic and transitions that carry real logical work are kept: the rewrites preserve Abstract/Intro/section flow and load-bearing connectives (however/therefore/by contrast; 然而/因此/相比之下), flattening only empty scaffolding.
cases.verification: Blind-judge semantic-fidelity dim 3 >= 4 (argument structure preserved) in worked notes; SKILL.md hard-constraint 4 + structural-statistical-signals.md A2 'keep transitions that do real logical work'. Would fail if structural SUBTRACT flattened genuine structure (fidelity would drop).

cases.covers_edge: already-clean prose -> near no-op, no artificial imperfections
cases.acceptance: Already-clean academic prose has ~zero lexical (0) and <=1 structural hits and is already bursty (sentence_cv > 0.2), so a correct rewrite is a near no-op; the skill does NOT over-edit or inject artificial imperfections.
cases.verification: behavioral::clean_prose_low_lexical/low_structural/already_bursty PASS; academic-register.md 'What not to add' (no deliberate imperfections).

cases.acceptance: A second rewrite pass over the output is near-no-op (no oscillation): the worked rewrites already have lexical_total 0 and structural_total 1, so the next pass finds nothing to remove; and the detector itself is idempotent (identical input -> identical map).
cases.verification: Detector on the worked rewrites shows lexical 0 / structural 1 (nothing left to thrash) + behavioral::detector_idempotent[en|zh] PASS + worked-notes Step 6 idempotency check.

cases.acceptance: The rewrites contain no slang/banter/jokes/fake-typos/emoji and no rhetorical-question flavor; output stays formal academic even though 'humanize' invites liveliness.
cases.verification: behavioral::register_no_casual[en|zh] + register_no_emoji[en|zh] PASS; blind-judge dim 2 (register) >= 4 and >= source in notes.

cases.acceptance: A 'just score this, don't rewrite' request returns the detector signal map and performs NO rewrite; the script is described as detecting, never humanizing.
cases.verification: behavioral::detect_only_returns_map + detect_only_help_says_detect_not_humanize PASS (CLI emits the 3-layer map; --help says 'DETECTS only — it never rewrites/humanizes'); SKILL.md Step 7.

cases.acceptance: A candidate that merely deletes denylist words but stays robotic/uniform must still be flagged AI-ish by the INDEPENDENT blind judge (it never sees the removal rules or detector). Success is independent of the skill's own pattern counts.
cases.verification: blind-judge-rubric.md is structurally blind (inputs = source + rewrite + dims only; denylist/detector withheld) and its pass thresholds require dim4 (texture added) >= 3 and dim1 >= 4, which a deletion-only/uniform rewrite fails; the marginal-lift check compares with/without-skill. Detector counts are explicitly excluded from the oracle. Would fail (collapse to closed loop) if the rubric used detector counts.
